description:
The cont-seam / halt-cont / spec-pollution work has uniformly been
*codegen-side hacks compensating for typer output we couldn't see*.
The annotated CLIF dump (fz-ul4.32.1) made the typer's view visible
INSIDE codegen output — useful, but still entangled with the
downstream layer.

We need a typer-output golden set: deterministic dumps of
`ModuleTypes` for a curated set of fixtures, snapshot-tested.
Independent of codegen entirely.

## Why this is load-bearing

The recent sequence of bugs (fz-jvo, fz-dhd, fz-h8w, fz-f6e, etc.)
were all "typer says X, codegen relies on Y, hack Y after the
fact." With a golden spec dump:

- We see typer output BEFORE writing codegen logic against it.
- Wrong specs are caught at the typer-tests level, not three
  layers down via runtime value mismatch.
- A typer change that affects fixture specs surfaces as a diff
  the reviewer reads, not a downstream test failure they
  triangulate.
- We can verify "this fixture's types are what we expect" without
  running any codegen.

## What the dump should contain

Per module:

- Every registered spec: `(FnId, key) → FnTypes`.
- For each spec: entry param Descrs (the key), return Descr (joined
  Term::Return + transitive TailCall via effective_return_descr),
  per-Var Descr from ft.vars, fn_constants entries.
- Cont input keys discovered at each Term::Call / CallClosure /
  Receive site (since those drive cont-spec registration).
- Sort everything deterministically (by FnId, then by key-string,
  then by Var ID).

## Format proposal

Text format, one spec per stanza:

  ; spec sum(2)
  ;   key:    [list(1|2|3|4|5)]
  ;   return: 0
  ;   vars:
  ;     Var(0) :: list(1|2|3|4|5)
  ;     Var(5) :: 1|2|3|4|5    [from ListHead(Var(0))]
  ;     ...
  ;   exits:
  ;     blk3 Return Var(1)      :: 0
  ;     blk5 Call sum(Var(6))    cont k_2 captured=[Var(5), Var(6)]
  ;                              cont_key=[0, 1|2|3|4|5, nil|list(1|2|3|4|5)]
  ;                              cont_resolves=k_2_s4

Goal: at a glance, can a human verify "yes that spec is what the
typer should produce for this fixture."

## Hook

`fz dump <src.fz> --emit specs` — new emit option alongside `clif`
and `asm`. Drives `type_module` and pretty-prints the result. No
codegen path involved.

## Acceptance

1. `fz dump <src.fz> --emit specs` produces a deterministic
   text dump of `ModuleTypes`.
2. A golden test set (mirrors fixtures/*.clif): `fixtures/*.specs`
   for the same curated fixture list, blessed and diff-tested.
3. Adding a fixture: `BLESS_SPECS=1 cargo test golden_specs`.
4. Tests assert: every registered spec accounted for; per-Var
   Descrs match; cont-site cont_keys match.
5. fact_5, add1, sum_list, map_higher_order, fact_chain — all
   blessed.
6. The dump is the FIRST thing a typer-touching ticket re-blesses;
   downstream CLIF / runtime tests come second.

## Implementation sketch

1. New `pretty_module_types(m: &Module, t: &ModuleTypes) -> String`
   in src/ir_typer.rs (or a sibling module).
2. Walks every spec in module_types.specs (sorted).
3. For each, formats key + ft.vars + per-block terminator info +
   cont site analysis (via cont_input_key per block).
4. New entry in src/main.rs `dump` dispatcher: `--emit specs` calls
   the pretty-printer.
5. New tests/golden_specs.rs (or extend fixture_matrix.rs) mirroring
   golden_clif's structure.
6. fixtures/<stem>.specs golden files.

## Out of scope

- Annotating the CLIF dump with specs (already done by fz-ul4.32.1).
  Golden specs is the separate-layer tool.
- Type-system reform. This is observability for the existing typer,
  not a new type system.

## Why high priority

Every typer-touching ticket from here on benefits from this. The
sequence fz-jvo → fz-dhd → fz-h8w cost real session time to
diagnose because we couldn't see typer output cleanly. This is the
feedback-loop foundation for the rest of the typer work (and there
is more — bottom-up topology, cont-chain seam, etc.).
title: fz-typer.golden-specs: standalone typer-output golden tests
